source.recording: Optimus _ EFL follow up  - 2026_06_30 11_03 EDT - Recording.mp4
source.notes: Optimus _ EFL follow up  - 2026_06_30 11_03 EDT - Notes by Gemini.docx

call_summary: Follow-up evaluation call in which Optimus rep Max Riemer walked EFL (Maggie Yepez, domestic head Neil, and largely-silent Alex Rosen) through the platform's domestic full-truckload freight intelligence, contrasting it with incumbent Revenue Vessel and covering data sources, lead-gen and account-management use cases, and HubSpot/TMS/Highway integration. Max presented a 15-month contract with a 3-month pilot at a 66% discount ($3,000/mo all-in, unlimited users) and a 90-day out. The call closed with Max to resend a clean pricing email and Maggie/Neil to review and respond before the end of next week.

delivery_engagement.explanation: Max was acoustically engaged (pitch variation 10.08 st, ~1.1 SD above his own baseline) and responsive in substance, tying the Highway partnership directly to Neil's stated procurement need and adapting pricing to EFL's unusual structure; the main weakness was preparedness (he couldn't recall the prior demo or locate the pricing email).

evidence.quote: that works the same way as a TMS integration where we sort of do the heavy lifting for you
I think that's along the lines of what you're saying, Neil
your situation would be a little bit different than what we normally do

value_prop_clarity.explanation: A neutral listener could explain the why: Optimus surfaces domestic FTL shippers on lanes where EFL already buys below market and has capacity, for both new logos and expanding existing accounts. The claims are concrete about lanes/buy-rates/capacity, though not backed by quantified ROI outcomes.

evidence.quote: surfacing lanes uh where you have capacity where you buy well
Identifying lanes where um you buy well where you know you are paying below the market average where you have extended capacity
let's go see what else they're moving that we haven't quoted or haven't seen

relevance.explanation: The pitch was clearly tailored to EFL: he referenced their Revenue Vessel/drayage setup, sized pricing to their forwarding-heavy business with a small brokerage arm, and probed their TMS and Highway status to shape the integration path.

evidence.quote: Y'all use revenue vessel for the dreage imports export side, right?
like the brokerage arm being, you know, not as big of a percentage of the full top line

discovery_progression.explanation: Max asked a few sharp qualifying questions (TMS, Highway, CRM), but he let the buyer drive discovery and closed softly—no booked next meeting, the pre-onboarding/ROI call left undated, and he actively defused urgency with a 'no rush' comment.

evidence.quote: We would want to want to discuss that on like a pre-onboarding call
I'll probably just send you a new email with everything regarding the pricing
I know the holiday's coming up, so no rush there

Max Riemer.coaching_action: Before ending the call, book a specific dated next step on the calendar—here, offer to schedule the pre-onboarding call to jointly define ROI success metrics and secure a decision date—instead of softening urgency and leaving the next move as 'resend the email and get back to me.'

signals.signal: Explicit own-words statement of interest and intent to discuss pricing
signals.quote: hey, we're interested and we want to know more as far as the pricing model

signals.signal: Buyer-owned, dated next step committing to a decision
signals.quote: I will get back to you before end of next week

signals.signal: Insisted on getting the domestic decision-maker on the call
signals.quote: I need him on this call to make sense of it for you and our time

signals.signal: Deep workflow-fit questioning, mapping the tool into their HubSpot process
signals.quote: This would allow Neil, I'm thinking, Alex, um, our salespeople to still work from HubSpot to pull leads. Is that true?

signals.signal: Endorsed the pilot structure and called it critical to proceeding
signals.quote: that trial period is would be critical for me

signals.signal: Articulated a concrete internal use case, showing he's envisioning adoption
signals.quote: how can I identify the lanes, the companies that make sense that would fit into our existing network
that's something that I would like to see, you know, a trial period

signals.signal: Raised a real adoption objection—significant new internal process required
signals.quote: we're going to have to create a whole new process around this

signals.signal: No questions and nothing to add when explicitly invited at close
Only substantive reaction was to the UI, not the value
Largely passive; early contributions were about connectivity/platform, not the deal
signals.quote: we are a Microsoft Teams company and we tend to struggle with the other platforms
